meta.title: BMW Z4 sDrive23i Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW Z4 sDrive23i engine — N52 2.5 straight-six. E89 generation, real UK costs and reliability. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

@graph.name: BMW Z4 sDrive23i Engine Era Map

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW Z4 sDrive23i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned N52B25 engine typically costs £1,800–£4,200 supply only. Fitting adds £800–£1,400. Total installed cost is typically £2,800–£5,000. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW Z4 sDrive23i engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The N52 is rated ★★★★☆ — it's widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable modern engines, with many owners reporting well over 200,000 miles with minimal issues. However, the electric water pump is a known weak point that will fail at some point, and oil leaks from gaskets are common.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW Z4 sDrive23i?

acceptedAnswer.text: Electric water pump failure (the number one issue), oil leaks from the valve cover and oil filter housing gaskets, and VANOS camshaft bearing ledge wear on high-mileage examples. Timing chain failures are almost unheard of on the N52.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW Z4 sDrive23i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Against a well-kept E89 Z4 sDrive23i worth £5,000–£12,000, a £3,000 recon is a manageable 25–60% of the vehicle's value — worth it for a well-maintained example. Given the Z4's classic roadster appeal, replacement is usually viable for vehicles in good condition.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW Z4 sDrive23i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The sDrive23i uses the N52B25 2.5-litre naturally aspirated inline-six engine. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N52's water pump, or does it need a new engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the water pump is a straightforward repair costing £500–£900. The N52's electric water pump is the most common service item on this engine; replacing it as a preventative measure at 80,000–100,000 miles is strongly recommended. If you notice overheating or coolant loss, book an inspection immediately — the pump can fail without warning.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW Z4 sDrive23i Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Z4 sDrive23i engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). BMW specialists typically charge £2,000–£3,500 for full engine replacement with a reconditioned unit.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW Z4 sDrive23i — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Catch it early before oil damages spark plugs
Preventative — before oil damages the serpentine belt
If caught early; specialist repair required
Very rare on N52; replace tensioner if noisy
Not a common N52 failure — if it occurs, replacement is the only option

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Z4 sDrive23i? Check These First
buyIf: * Full BMW service history with documented oil-change intervals
* Water pump replaced at or before 100,000 miles — this is the number one preventative item
* Evidence of valve cover and oil filter housing gasket replacements
* Low-mileage example with full running-in service completed
avoidIf: * Overheating history with no record of water pump replacement
* Oil leaks with no record of repair
* Rough idle or hesitation — potential VANOS issues
* Engine warning light present with no diagnosis documentation

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Z4 sDrive23i's N52 is one of BMW's most reliable modern engines — a smooth, free-revving naturally aspirated straight-six that's a joy to drive. The N52 is known for clocking well over 200,000 miles with minimal issues. However, like any engine, it has its quirks: electric water pump failure, oil leaks, and VANOS wear on high-mileage cars. Buy on service history and proof of preventative water pump replacement. If you're replacing, a reconditioned unit with a 12-month warranty is the sweet spot.

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW Z4 sDrive23i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Coolant System — Has the electric water pump been replaced? This is the number one N52 weak point
* Cold Start — Listen for any rattle on startup (timing chain issues are rare on N52, but check anyway)
* Service History — Including which engine era (N52B25 is the only sDrive23i engine — verify this before purchase)
* Oil Leaks — Check for oil spots under the car or a burning oil smell (valve cover or oil filter housing gaskets)
* Rough Idle — Any hesitation or rough running? VANOS wear indicator
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 5,000 miles? If so, investigate
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved VANOS or cooling issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures; the N52 should pass easily if well-maintained

items.label: * Valvetrain
items.value: DOHC with Valvetronic and Double VANOS
